pins.name: /PL
pins.desc: parallel load, active LOW; asynchronous on the real part, modeled synchronously (FR-079c)

pins.name: /CE
pins.desc: clock enable, active LOW (a HIGH inhibits CP)

pins.name: Q7
pins.desc: serial output from the last stage

pins.name: Q7N
pins.desc: complementary output from the last stage (physical pin /Q7)

typeData.behavior:
; 74165 — 8-bit parallel-in/serial-out shift register. Derived from the
; function table (datasheet Table 3, verified against the PDF).
;
; SEQUENTIAL-PART CLOCK CONVENTION (as on the 74574): GALasm's .R registered
; outputs are clocked by the GAL22V10's dedicated pin-1 clock. Here that role
; is played by CP, the LOW->HIGH edge-triggered clock pin. CP therefore does
; NOT appear as a term in any equation below; the .R suffix implies it.
;
; BURIED SHIFT STAGES (FR-079c): the register is eight bits deep but exposes
; only the last stage. SR0..SR6 (declared under internal:) are the seven hidden
; flip-flops; Q7 is the eighth, exposed on pin Q7. Each is a .R register that
; latches on CP; the buried nodes drive no pin. A shift is written as a chain
; SRi.R = ... SR(i-1): because a buried node rides the one-unit-delay model
; exactly like a net, reading SR(i-1) yields its pre-edge value, so the chain
; advances exactly one stage per clock (FR-079c).
;
; Equations are physical-level GALasm: '/' is the complement operator and a
; signal name is this file's pin name with any active-low '/' prefix dropped.
; So 'PL' is pin /PL and '/PL' means "pin /PL is LOW" (load asserted); 'CE' is
; pin /CE and '/CE' means "pin /CE is LOW" (clock enabled). Each stage's D input
; selects among three modes on the clock edge:
;   /PL*Dn       — LOAD:  /PL LOW captures the parallel input;
;   PL*/CE*<in>  — SHIFT: /PL HIGH and /CE LOW takes the previous stage
;                         (the serial input DS for the first stage);
;   PL*CE*<self> — HOLD:  /PL HIGH and /CE HIGH (clock inhibit) retains the bit,
;                         so an inhibited clock edge does not disturb the state.
;
; TWO DELIBERATE FIDELITY APPROXIMATIONS (FR-079c/FR-079a, design section 8):
; (1) parallel load is modeled SYNCHRONOUSLY (it rides a CP edge while /PL is
;     LOW) whereas the real part loads asynchronously — the engine has no
;     variable async-load primitive. (2) the clock is CP alone with /CE folded
;     in as a synchronous hold, so the datasheet's secondary "shift on /CE's
;     rising edge while CP is LOW" path is not reproduced. The dominant
;     behaviors (eight-stage shift, /CE inhibit-as-hold, load-then-serialize)
;     are faithful.
;
; Q7N is the complementary output. It is NOT named /Q7: the signal-name rule
; strips the '/', so Q7 and /Q7 would collide on one net. It is driven by the
; derived inverse of the Q7 net (Q7N = /Q7), lagging Q7 by one unit.
SR0.R = /PL*D0 + PL*/CE*DS + PL*CE*SR0
SR1.R = /PL*D1 + PL*/CE*SR0 + PL*CE*SR1
SR2.R = /PL*D2 + PL*/CE*SR1 + PL*CE*SR2
SR3.R = /PL*D3 + PL*/CE*SR2 + PL*CE*SR3
SR4.R = /PL*D4 + PL*/CE*SR3 + PL*CE*SR4
SR5.R = /PL*D5 + PL*/CE*SR4 + PL*CE*SR5
SR6.R = /PL*D6 + PL*/CE*SR5 + PL*CE*SR6
Q7.R  = /PL*D7 + PL*/CE*SR6 + PL*CE*Q7
Q7N = /Q7
